item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is very funny, and the discovery is too funny to be forgotten.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How does that flare gun work? Other than that this is a great article IMO.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the concept of putting the joke about a banana being a knife and/or a gun to the extreme :3 (Though i woud like to see what happens when it is a flaregun).

+1 ~(=w=)

item.title: Almost 4 years later
item.content:encoded: I still come back to this because people seem to enjoy it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
only to witness the suspect fatally slash his victim with the banana

Glad I'm not the only one that immediately upvoted at that line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a holdover from an earlier time on the site. "Amnesiacs" was the term used at the very start of the site, "amnestics" is the right term, but some authors prefer to use "amnesiacs" as a stylistic choice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a good idea, but there's a slight typo where "amnesiacs" should be "amnestics".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree, this is the best line, like BANANA KNIFE, DIIIIIIIIE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
only to witness the suspect fatally slash his victim with the banana

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno if I can necro my own SCP, but it's cool yall enjoy this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
only to witness the suspect fatally slash his victim with the banana, causing death due to a severed jugular vein.

Aaaaand I upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this scp deserves more attention than it's getting. +1 ^-^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The imagery in this made me chuckle. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoy this. It's simple.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha, thank you. This is my first SCP and it's +30. Not a +25892589852 like some but I'm glad I could make a good contribution to the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Is that a banana in your pocket or are you just— holy shit, did you just cut a bitch with a fruit?"

I love this. I love this so much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On the fence until the whole "holding someone hostage with a banana" gag went R-rated… that is fucking awesome! The only thing I would've like better is if he blew the hostage's head off with a banana flare gun!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure it you want any Adam West Batman references, but Ballpoint Pen would be a great function.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Originally, I had the first page be an explanation of "what it is".

And thanks for that, Wishun. I'll change it up now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The chip and second page of SCP-1417-2's anomalous properties manifest when the chip is placed upon the page, which is a chart containing several options, accompanied by a blank box.

This sentence is badly fractured and unclear. I think a complete rewording is in order. How about: "The second page contains a chart of several options with accompanying blank boxes. Anomalous properties manifest when the chip is placed on one of the boxes."

Also, what's on the first page? Why two pages at all, since the first seems so not worth mentioning?

The section on its indestructibility seems overly-detailed when you could have just said "indestructible" and moved on. On the other hand, the technical description distracts from the cliche so it might be the lesser evil.

All in all, I liked it. Very odd, yet internally consistent in some way I can't put my finger on. Good job, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lefty seems like he has a lot of potential.
If there is still an effort to port stuff back and forth, can I request Lefty get a spot on the translation list?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nicely underplayed, all the way along. When you brought out 'fatally slashed his victim with the banana' I burst out laughing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WHY DIDN'T I THINK OF THIS Needs more raspberries.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better. It totally works without us knowing who made it, only that it was intentionally made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess I slipped up here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Relevant skit: http://youtu.be/piWCBOsJr-w

"How to arm yourself against a man armed with a banana"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: godammit, i'm leaving the forum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I meant by limped appearance refers to how when fruit starts to go bad it can get all soggy and stuff, and since a banana is, well, in the shape it is, I've seen rotten bananas go limpy and sag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can't say I find this banana very appealing. I fixed a few slips, but overall your efforts to get my +1 have failed to bear fruit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Saw "SWISS ARMY BANANA"
Upvoted
Read article
Left upvote where it was

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not entirely sure how I feel about this - I'm not usually a fan of very silly skips but I do like the premise here. No vote for the minute - possibly it'll, er, grow on me later. Worth flagging up you still refer to a 'limped appearance' which doesn't really make sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ooh, I like that.

Here's what I'm thinking so far:

Alternatively, if an individual signs his or her name in the "REGISTERED OWNER" text-box, SCP-1417-1 will respond to voice commands, sofar as they match the commands found in SCP-1417-2. For example, saying or yelling "REST" whilst in the presence of SCP-1417-1 will cause the item to enter into rest mode. Either method(the chip or signing the form) has been found to be effective at manipulating SCP-1417-1. Further testing is underway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll admit now that I've never watched Monty Python, so…if it was, it was unintentional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was sorely disappointed when I realised that the whole article had been a setup for a Monty Python reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sign your name on the form, in the space that says "registered owner". You are now the registered owner of the banana. It will cange state in response to voice commands from the registered owner, and no one else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea is for the user to put the chip into the box, and then it's there until the user decides to move it.

If you have an alternate suggestion, please let me know. Doing my best to do some cleaning up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sounds like a weird video game item. It would be right at home in Problem Sleuth.

Ecological analysis reveals that SCP-1417-1 does not differ from an ordinary banana in any way, with its skin and exposed flesh consistent with ecological norms.

I think you mean "biological", not "ecological".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
fatally slash his victim with the banana

Oh boy

-

That aside, this is a solid idea with kinda shaky execution. The idea of a chip on a piece of paper just seem kinda… awkward. Like, it's from a crazy guy, and it just seems weird for him to move a chip around calmly.

IDK, I would say yelling or pointing works better but that's up to you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Off the top of my head:

SCP-1417 is a collective designation for two objects which possess shared and related anomalous properties.

This is kinda awkward and vague, especially for the first sentence. Suggest rewording it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What would you recommend?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that was my bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sometimes no context is better than a weak one. MFM is still a mysterious corporation that produces anomalies, and we really have enough of those.

To provide you with an alternate, this seems like it fits with the modus operandi of Lefty, a character from the Russian wiki who supplies criminals with anomalous tools to pull off various felonies.

EDIT: I see you removed the MFM reference. That works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this enough not to downvote, but I feel it needs a little more cleanup 'fore I can +1 it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think people are being misled by the fact that in the first page of comments you stated that MfM was a competitor to the Factory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally I'd rather it be "meh" then total rubbish, so I take that as a compliment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This strikes me as very meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There we go. I removed the letter. Wasn't a big deal, I just intended it to provide context is all.

Apologies for the confusion and stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Again, it wasn't really meant as a Factory competitor. I was just trying to give the object a bit of context. If you guys don't like the first page, I'll remove it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The 'banana that changes properties' is a decent idea, but I don't really care for the execution. Or for the Factory competitor — just makes me sigh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It wasn't really meant as a GoI, it was just giving the product context.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's pretty much it. MfM isn't meant as a new GoI, it was just context.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't really like the inclusion of any sort of context for the creation, I liked it better when this was just a banana I could stab someone with. That, combined with some tone issues, is why I'm downvoting. You had my attention, but then the more I read the less interested I became, and then that final note at the end made me roll my eyes. It's just the same end note I've seen so many times before.

Removed the note, so I removed my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Factory doesn't really have competitors. It might be a competitor to Wondertainment, or Prometheus Labs, but, even then, It's a dumb idea. We've got so many GOI, it's ridiculous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think the intention here was to try to make a new GoI, just to give it some context, because this really doesn't fit the MO of any of the current "salesman" GoI's except perhaps Wondertainment, and even that one's a bit of a stretch. I would personally be surprised if there were only 2-4 sellers of anomalous items in the SCP'verse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not too hot on the creation of yet another GoI, but the object itself is fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I shouldn't like this, but I do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for that - sometimes, precise wording is a bit of pain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I figured that's what you meant. Say "developed a limp appearance" and it will say what you mean.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
By "limp" I mean how fruit goes soggy sometimes when it's rotten and tends to sag.

Thanks to those who've edited my errors thusfar. Big help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Normally I hate bananas, but I kind of like this one. However, your banana needs editing. Fix the stuff Vincent pointed out, and the bit where your banana "develops a limp" — that implies it was walking around and stepped on a tack… Or maybe someone kneecapped it for misusing "it's."
